input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 9 (and earlier), Android used `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to resize the window when the keyboard appeared. However, `Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ and was removed/stopped working in .NET 10.

The recommended approach in .NET 10 is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle edge-to-edge/insets properly.


The simplest solution is to rely on `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which MAUI enables by default to automatically scroll content when the keyboard appears, replacing the deprecated `Resize` behavior.
